all_vocabulary.meaning: a speaking style where words are delivered in a choppy, disconnected manner
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: She was falling victim to staccato style speaking when she spoke like this.

all_vocabulary.meaning: a speaking style where words flow smoothly and are connected together
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Use legato style speaking which means all of the words are linked together.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the clear and distinct pronunciation of words and sounds
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: There is nothing wrong with your accent but there's a lot wrong with pronunciation and articulation.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Speaking in a confused or lengthy way without clear direction
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Instead of rambling when asked questions, use a framework to structure your response

all_vocabulary.meaning: A structured approach or template for organizing thoughts and responses
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: When you have a framework, you fall back on the framework instead of panicking

all_vocabulary.meaning: Speaking spontaneously without preparation
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: If I have to speak off the cuff, I'm much less confident than with prepared content

all_vocabulary.meaning: The auditory component of your voice and how it sounds to others
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Audit the auditory component of your voice known as your vocal image.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Non-words like 'um', 'ah', 'you know' that interrupt speech clarity
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Bring all your non-words and filler words to the forefront of your mind.

all_vocabulary.meaning: done without being planned or rehearsed
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: The speech must be impromptu, not planned or rehearsed.

all_vocabulary.meaning: extremely painful or unpleasant
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: This is an excruciating process when you watch yourself on video.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the quality of being trusted and believed in
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Fill words rob you of credibility and authority.

all_vocabulary.meaning: practical and realistic
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Share with me the most pragmatic lessons you've learned

all_vocabulary.meaning: concentrated the essence of something
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: She distilled for me liquid gold that took her 20 years to learn

all_vocabulary.meaning: thorough research and investigation
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: I've researched you and done my due diligence

all_vocabulary.meaning: The rhythm or flow of speech energy
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Wrong melody, wrong sequence of energy when introducing yourself.

all_vocabulary.meaning: The order or pattern of energy in communication
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Getting the right sequence of energy is crucial for successful introductions.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the study of personal space and distances in human interaction
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Understanding proxemic helps you position yourself correctly on video calls.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the closest personal distance zone, reserved for very close relationships
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Being too close to the camera puts you in someone's intimate space, making them uncomfortable.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the distance zone for close friends and family interactions
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Personal space is still within arm's reach but feels less invasive than intimate space.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the appropriate distance zone for professional and social interactions
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Position yourself in social space on video calls so your hand gestures are visible and effective.

all_vocabulary.meaning: lacking in variety and interest; tediously uniform
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Even engaging speech becomes monotonous without vocal variety.

all_vocabulary.meaning: having a strong effect or influence
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Slow default speech is neither influential nor impactful.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Having the ability to make decisions quickly and effectively
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Reds are decisive communicators who prefer clear, direct outcomes.

all_vocabulary.meaning: In communication context, being able to speak multiple communication styles or 'colors'
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Masterful communicators are multilingual without losing their identity.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Characterized by agreement, peace, and avoiding conflict
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Greens prefer harmonious environments and resist sudden changes.

all_vocabulary.meaning: mentally exhausted or confused
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Your brain becomes frazzled when you try to think and speak simultaneously

all_vocabulary.meaning: a state of mental confusion or disorganization
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Making it up as you go causes scatter brain

all_vocabulary.meaning: A hormone released during stress that causes physical symptoms like shaking
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Excess adrenaline is the main physiological reason why we shake when nervous.

all_vocabulary.meaning: The body's natural stress response that prepares you to either face a threat or escape from it
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: The body is preparing for fight or flight when you're about to speak publicly.

all_vocabulary.meaning: A clear, definitive statement that expresses something directly
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Timing and consistency with our social media content will directly impact engagement.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Growing exponentially where gains build upon previous gains
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Compounding interest is like rolling a snowball downhill - it gets bigger and faster as it goes.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Creating a deep connection or meaningful impact with your audience
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: You go from rambling to resonating when you use clear frameworks.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the impression people form based on your physical appearance and presentation
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: We focus on our visual image when we wear a suit or beautiful dress to meet clients

all_vocabulary.meaning: relating to deep breathing using the diaphragm muscle
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: We want to take diaphragmatic breaths otherwise we can waste a lot of the oxygen

all_vocabulary.meaning: mutual trust and friendship among people
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: It's been amazing to see the camaraderie that you have with the team

all_vocabulary.meaning: the vocal quality expressing emotion and attitude
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: You must keep that caring tonality moving forward when giving feedback

all_vocabulary.meaning: having compelling charm or appeal that inspires devotion in others
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Using proper body language can make you more charismatic when speaking.

all_vocabulary.meaning: the range of hand and body movements you use to communicate
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Playing charades helps increase your gesture vocabulary for more expressive communication.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Creating multiple conversation paths or topics within a single response
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Conversational threading helps keep discussions flowing naturally.

all_vocabulary.meaning: Away from others; isolated or private
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: I prefer secluded areas because they help me focus on creative work.

all_vocabulary.meaning: awkward or ungraceful in movement or expression
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: In the beginning when you do this it's going to feel really clunky

all_vocabulary.meaning: a standard or usual way of doing something
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Any default rate of speech is bad for maintaining audience attention

all_vocabulary.meaning: speaking in a way that is not clear because the mouth is not opened properly
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: When we don't move our jaw then all time seems like we're mumbling

all_vocabulary.meaning: a vocal exercise where you vibrate your lips together
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Lip trills helps you get rid of the morning voice

all_vocabulary.meaning: British slang for an unpleasant or obnoxious person
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: If I spoke to you like this you'd think I'm a bit of a wanker

all_vocabulary.meaning: to change how you think about or approach a situation
all_vocabulary.example_sentence: Stop reframe not fake and phony it's just unfamiliar

all_frameworks.name: Accent Improvement Method
steps: Record a 20-minute video of yourself speaking
Use ChatGPT to get top 20 questions to ask someone you've just met
Answer those questions on camera
Send video to speech pathologist
Get feedback on specific sounds you're making incorrectly
Practice the 10 actionable sounds and mouth movements

all_frameworks.name: 3-2-1 Framework
steps: Three steps (give three steps to do something)
Two types (identify two categories or ways)
One thing (share the most important point)

all_frameworks.name: Record and Review Process
steps: Record a 5-minute improvised video
Wait until the next day to review
First review: audio only - evaluate vocal image
Second review: video with sound off - evaluate body language and facial expressions
Third review: get transcription including filler words - evaluate verbal clarity

all_frameworks.name: Three-Step Video Review Process
steps: Record 5-minute impromptu video
Auditory review - listen with sound up, take notes on vocal foundations
Visual review - watch on mute, observe body language
Get transcript and highlight nonwords/fill words with red highlighter

all_frameworks.name: Expert Knowledge Acquisition Method
steps: Research the best experts in your field
Email them politely with context
Offer to pay their monthly rate for intensive learning
Ask for distilled lessons from their years of experience
Prepare for concentrated knowledge transfer

all_frameworks.name: Networking Conversation Entry
steps: Use humorous, apologetic energy
Express genuine interest in their conversation
Ask permission to join
Read their facial expressions for welcome
Exit gracefully if unwelcome

all_frameworks.name: Proxemic Zones for Video Calls
steps: Avoid intimate space (too close to camera)
Don't position in personal space (within arm's reach)
Position in social space for optimal presence
Ensure hand gestures are visible

all_frameworks.name: Four Color Communication System
steps: Identify if person is introverted or extroverted
Determine if they are logical or emotional
Observe if they speak deliberately or quickly
Categorize into Red, Yellow, Green, or Blue
Adapt communication style accordingly

all_frameworks.name: Red Communication Framework
steps: Be direct and decisive
Skip background details
Present top 3 key points
Recommend clear next steps
Ask for questions

all_frameworks.name: Pause Before Speaking
steps: Pause before you start talking
Give yourself a moment to think
Visualize the path of your response
Then speak with clarity

all_frameworks.name: Pre-Speech Adrenaline Management
steps: Go for a brisk walk for 3-5 minutes backstage
Do Wim Hof breathing exercises
Perform 20 star jumps
Do 5 push-ups
Take deep breaths

all_frameworks.name: CCC Framework
steps: Context - Set the scene and give the why before the what
Core - Deliver one clear main idea
Connect - Show why it matters to them

all_frameworks.name: Vocal Warm-up Routine
steps: Do lip trills on one note for a full minute
Lip trill your favorite song
Do lip trills in short bursts for a minute

all_frameworks.name: Volume and Melody Technique
steps: Increase volume as needed
Add melodic variation to avoid harshness
End statements on a low pitch for authority

all_frameworks.name: Sandwich Technique
steps: Start with something positive
Frame the feedback with care
Give the specific feedback
End with something positive

all_frameworks.name: Five Body Language Hacks for Charisma
steps: Use computer gesture while listening
Match facial expressions to words
Practice charades to expand gesture vocabulary
Apply triangle technique for eye contact
Keep gestures in the Power Sphere

all_frameworks.name: Triangle Technique
steps: Look at left eye for 4-5 seconds
Shift to right eye for 4-5 seconds
Move focus to nose bridge
Return to original eye

all_frameworks.name: Conversational Threading
steps: Receive a question
Prepare response with multiple topics/threads
Include personal details that spark different interests
Allow the other person to choose which thread to pursue

all_frameworks.name: Random Word Speaking Exercise
steps: Go to a random word generator
Generate a random word
Talk about that word for 1-2 minutes
Practice this 1-4 times per day
Accept that it will feel clunky initially
Continue practicing to strengthen mind-mouth connection

all_frameworks.name: Speech Rate Variation
steps: Go quickly when showing passion
Slow down when explaining something new
Vary rate back and forth to maintain engagement

all_frameworks.name: Five Facial Warmup Exercises
steps: Make your face big and small for 30 seconds
Pretend to chew a really big piece of gum
Move your eyebrows up and down
Do lip trills
Move your jaw from side to side

all_frameworks.name: Voice Development Process
steps: Recognize your voice can change
Understand voice reflects personality
Explore vocal range like an instrument
Accept unfamiliarity as growth
Practice consistently

all_principles.title: The Saxophone Analogy
all_principles.explanation: Just as learning a musical instrument feels awkward at first but improves with practice, new vocal and speaking techniques will feel unfamiliar initially but become natural over time

all_principles.title: Practice Creates Familiarity
all_principles.explanation: Current communication behaviors feel natural because they've been practiced for decades, while new techniques feel fake simply because they're new and unfamiliar

all_principles.title: Overdo Mouth Movements
all_principles.explanation: Exaggerate your mouth movements when speaking to improve articulation and pronunciation clarity

all_principles.title: Volume Overcomes Shame
all_principles.explanation: Increasing your vocal volume and stepping into courage makes shame invisible to your audience

all_principles.title: Link Words Together
all_principles.explanation: Use legato style speaking to connect words smoothly rather than speaking in a choppy, disconnected manner

all_principles.title: Framework Over Panic
all_principles.explanation: When unprepared, rely on communication frameworks instead of letting your brain scramble and cause rambling

all_principles.title: Preparation Builds Confidence
all_principles.explanation: Having frameworks makes you excited to communicate rather than scared, improving both content and body language

all_principles.title: Mental Organization Under Pressure
all_principles.explanation: Frameworks prevent your brain from opening multiple thought processes simultaneously, keeping you focused

all_principles.title: Three-Part Analysis
all_principles.explanation: Analyze your speaking performance through three separate lenses: auditory (vocal image), visual (body language), and verbal (word choice and filler words)

all_principles.title: Delayed Self-Review
all_principles.explanation: Don't review your recording immediately as you are too self-critical; wait until the next day for objective analysis

all_principles.title: Self-Awareness Through Recording
all_principles.explanation: Recording yourself speaking and reviewing it builds awareness of how others perceive you, which changes how you come across

all_principles.title: Separate Audio and Visual Analysis
all_principles.explanation: Analyzing vocal elements separately from visual elements provides clearer feedback on specific areas for improvement

all_principles.title: Value Expert Time Appropriately
all_principles.explanation: Pay professionals their worth to gain access to years of distilled experience in a condensed format

all_principles.title: Seek Shortcuts Through Expertise
all_principles.explanation: Instead of learning through trial and error over decades, invest in learning from those who have already mastered the field

all_principles.title: Always hold something when networking
all_principles.explanation: Holding a drink gives you something to do with your hands and provides an easy exit strategy from conversations

all_principles.title: Read the room before entering
all_principles.explanation: Pay attention to facial expressions and body language to determine if you're welcome in a conversation

all_principles.title: Have graceful exit strategies
all_principles.explanation: Prepare polite ways to leave conversations, whether they reject you or become boring

all_principles.title: Social Space Positioning
all_principles.explanation: Position yourself in social space during video calls to make others comfortable and allow your hand gestures to be visible, creating more influential and engaging communication

all_principles.title: Vocal Variety Prevention
all_principles.explanation: Avoid getting stuck in a default rate of speech by consciously varying your pace, as consistent pacing leads to disengagement regardless of whether it's too slow or too fast

all_principles.title: Engagement Through Variation
all_principles.explanation: What initially seems engaging becomes monotonous over time without variety, making vocal variation essential for sustained audience attention

all_principles.title: Adapt Your Communication Style
all_principles.explanation: Most people communicate in their own color, but effective communicators learn to speak all four colors to connect with different personality types

all_principles.title: Dynamic Like Water
all_principles.explanation: Be adaptable in your communication approach while maintaining your core identity, adjusting to fit the communication vessel you're poured into

all_principles.title: Everyone Has All Colors
all_principles.explanation: We all possess aspects of each communication color but tend to default to one primary style

all_principles.title: Think Before You Speak
all_principles.explanation: Instead of pausing mid-sentence when lost, pause before speaking to organize your thoughts and see the path clearly

all_principles.title: Embrace Strategic Pauses
all_principles.explanation: Don't be afraid to pause before answering - it's better than scrambling for words while speaking

all_principles.title: Address the Root Cause
all_principles.explanation: Instead of trying to manage symptoms on stage, eliminate the excess adrenaline that causes shaking before you speak

all_principles.title: Physical Preparation for Mental Performance
all_principles.explanation: Use physical exercises backstage to burn off nervous energy and regulate your physiological state

all_principles.title: Pauses create confidence
all_principles.explanation: People who pause more are perceived as being more confident, not less. Pauses give your ideas space to land.

all_principles.title: Volume equals energy transfer
all_principles.explanation: When you speak with healthy volume, you're not just sharing information, you're transferring energy. People feel your conviction.

all_principles.title: Analogies are shortcuts to understanding
all_principles.explanation: They connect the unknown to the known by taking something complex and tying it to something simple and familiar.

all_principles.title: Your voice is a muscle system
all_principles.explanation: Like any muscle, if you go from cold to full exertion without warming up, you'll strain it and it will underperform.

all_principles.title: Gender Dynamics in Speaking
all_principles.explanation: Women face different challenges than men when projecting authority - men can get away with forceful delivery while women may be perceived negatively for the same approach

all_principles.title: Melody Prevents Harshness
all_principles.explanation: Adding vocal melody and variation when increasing volume prevents sounding angry or aggressive

all_principles.title: Low Pitch for Authority
all_principles.explanation: Ending statements on a low pitch rather than high pitch maintains credibility and authority

all_principles.title: Vocal Image Creates First Impressions
all_principles.explanation: People make assumptions about your personality traits like friendliness and intelligence based on how you sound, not just how you look

all_principles.title: Connection Before Influence
all_principles.explanation: You must first establish genuine connection with people before you have earned the right to influence their decisions

all_principles.title: Deliberate Vocal Development
all_principles.explanation: Just as we consciously manage our visual appearance for professional settings, we must intentionally develop our vocal presentation

all_principles.title: Never give feedback in public
all_principles.explanation: Always pull someone aside for private conversations to avoid hitting their ego in front of peers

all_principles.title: Maintain caring tonality throughout
all_principles.explanation: Keep the same supportive vocal tone from beginning to end, don't switch to harsh criticism mid-conversation

all_principles.title: Frame feedback with supportive intent
all_principles.explanation: Explain that your goal is to help them succeed before delivering critical observations

all_principles.title: Facial Expression Congruence
all_principles.explanation: Your facial expressions must match the emotional content of your words to maintain authenticity and trust

all_principles.title: Power Sphere Positioning
all_principles.explanation: Gesturing between the belly button and eyes creates higher levels of charisma and influence compared to lower positioning

all_principles.title: Active Listening Through Body Language
all_principles.explanation: The computer gesture demonstrates engagement and care when someone else is speaking

all_principles.title: Give Multiple Conversation Options
all_principles.explanation: Instead of giving short, dead-end answers, include several different topics in your response so others have multiple directions to take the conversation

all_principles.title: Practice Prepared Responses
all_principles.explanation: Anticipate common questions and prepare responses that naturally include multiple conversational threads

all_principles.title: Mind and Mouth Connection
all_principles.explanation: The ability to quickly translate thoughts into spoken words - when weak, you think well but struggle to verbalize spontaneously

all_principles.title: Progressive Practice Tolerance
all_principles.explanation: Accept that initial attempts will feel awkward and clunky as part of the natural learning process

all_principles.title: Strategic Speech Pacing
all_principles.explanation: Speaking too quickly or too slowly both cause problems - the key is to strategically vary your rate based on content and desired emotional impact

all_principles.title: Facial Muscle Activation
all_principles.explanation: Warming up facial muscles before speaking helps prevent mumbling and improves vocal clarity by engaging all the muscles needed for clear articulation

all_principles.title: Jaw Movement for Clarity
all_principles.explanation: Dropping and moving the jaw properly is essential for clear speech - without jaw movement, speech becomes mumbled and unclear

all_principles.title: Voice as Behavior
all_principles.explanation: Your voice is not fixed but consists of behaviors that can be modified and improved through practice

all_principles.title: Voice as Instrument
all_principles.explanation: Like a piano, your voice has full range of keys to explore rather than using just one monotone note

all_principles.title: Unfamiliarity vs Fakeness
all_principles.explanation: When developing new vocal skills, discomfort comes from unfamiliarity, not from being fake or phony

all_principles.title: Communication Determines Perception
all_principles.explanation: People perceive your competence based on your communication skills, not your technical abilities alone

all_example_sentences.sentence: The one thing about [topic] is [key point]
There are two types/ways: [option 1] and [option 2]
Three steps to [topic]: first [step 1], second [step 2], third [step 3]
My name's Vinh, I'd love to learn from you, I know you've been in this industry for 20 years and I've done my due diligence, you're one of the best
Oh hi hi how you going sorry to bother you I'm Vin great to meet you all
I can tell this is sensitive it's okay
I'm out of water be right back
Oh hey oh my goodness so good to see you
Hi everyone it's great to be here
Really good to be here can't wait to take you through vocal foundations it's going to be amazing
Really excited to be here to take you through the vocal foundations for the next couple of days
The report's finished. I've gone through all the data and summarized the top three points and here's what I recommend the next steps be.
Just hit the record button. Go have some fun. Go work it out as you go. You're going to love it.
I know this is a shift, but we'll move at a pace that feels comfortable for you, and I'm going to support you all the way through it.
Here's all the data, and here's the step-by-step plan that I've created from the data, and I want to give it to you so you can do a quick double check if you want.
When I think about spirituality I really believe that there are different chapters in life
Your voice, it matters. Communication, it's not just a skill, it's a superpower.
Hey boss, as you know, we're about to release the new online course and the timing is tight.
Compounding interest is like rolling a snowball downhill - at first it's small, but as it rolls, it picks up more snow.
Hi everyone, are you ready to start day one?
It's been amazing to see the camaraderie that you have, I love that you're checking everybody
I want to help every single student in this diving team succeed, could I share with you a few thoughts
Someone like yourself is doing amazing, you're really contributing to the team
I'm from Adelaide, I live up in Golfview Heights - it's kind of a hilly area because I love the greenery and it just inspires me to be creative, I create a lot of content online
Good morning I'm so excited to be here I can't wait to talk about perspective influence and beliefs
Good morning everyone... I'm so excited... to spend the next hour...
